message:
fix(kafka): close TOCTOU race between rebalance and close via double-checked locking

KafkaConsumer::rebalance now re-checks the closed flag under the state
lock after passing the initial open guard, preventing a race where
close() sets the flag between the open check and the state mutation.
Previously, a concurrent close() could succeed while rebalance() was
between ensure_open() and the state lock acquisition, allowing the
rebalance to modify partition assignments on a consumer that was already
logically closed.

The fix adds a second closed.load(Acquire) inside the locked section.
If the consumer was closed in the window between the open check and the
lock acquisition, rebalance returns KafkaError::Config("consumer is
closed") without modifying state, preserving the invariant that closed
consumers have stable partition assignments.

Test infrastructure: adds a RebalanceAfterOpenHook that uses two
std::sync::Barriers to pause the rebalance thread after the open check
but before the lock acquisition, allowing the test to close() the
consumer in the precise window and verify the race is rejected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
